name: Spindalidae
description: Spindalidae is a family of colorful passerine birds found in the Caribbean islands. Formerly placed with the tanagers, they are now recognized as a distinct family endemic to the Greater Antilles and the Bahamas.

children.name: Spindalis
children.description: A genus of brightly colored birds with bold head patterns, endemic to the Caribbean islands.

children.name: Spindalis zena
children.description: The Western Spindalis is a strikingly colored bird with a black-and-white striped head, yellow-orange breast, and olive back. It is found in the Bahamas, Cuba, and the Cayman Islands.

children.name: Spindalis nigricephala
children.description: The Jamaican Spindalis has a black head with white stripes, a bright yellow breast, and olive-green upperparts. It is endemic to Jamaica, inhabiting forests and woodlands across the island.

children.name: Spindalis dominicensis
children.description: The Hispaniolan Spindalis has a black-and-white striped head, orange-yellow underparts, and olive upperparts. It is endemic to the island of Hispaniola.

children.name: Spindalis portoricensis
children.description: The Puerto Rican Spindalis has a black head with white stripes, yellow breast, and olive back. It is endemic to Puerto Rico and is the national bird of the island.
